snippet.title: iPhone 17 Pro Max vs Samsung S25 Ultra – Most Detailed Comparison
snippet.description:
Two giants, one ultimate showdown — the iPhone 17 Pro Max vs Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra! 💥

In this real-world flagship comparison, we test build quality, display, performance, battery life, cameras, thermals, AI features, software experience, and resale value — everything that matters when spending ₹1.5L+.


Google Drive Link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Yy98vQ2NtwizA5U2WtKcY7FeT7TFWTHp?usp=sharing


📱 iPhone 17 Pro Max Highlights:

- Apple A19 Pro chip (3nm) with vapor-chamber cooling
- Super Retina XDR OLED, 3000-nits brightness
- Apple Intelligence AI + iOS 26
- ProRes, Log2, and Genlock support for professional video creators
- Up to 39 hrs playback, MagSafe + Qi2 wireless charging

⚡ Galaxy S25 Ultra Highlights:

- Snapdragon 8 Elite for Galaxy + massive vapor-chamber cooling
- QHD+ Dynamic AMOLED 2X display with Vision Booster
- 200MP main + 50MP 5× periscope camera system
- S Pen productivity, fast 45W charging, and Galaxy AI features

snippet.title: Vivo X300 Pro vs iPhone 17 Pro - Big Camera Comparison and Review!
snippet.description:
In this video, I’m testing the Vivo X300 Pro against the iPhone 17 Pro: two of the most powerful camera phones of 2025. 

This video is sponsored by ANKER.
Get yours Anker Nano charging series goodies to perfectly match your new iPhone 17:
Anker Nano Power Bank (5K,MagGo, Slim): https://ankerfast.club/wekaxg 
Anker Nano Charging Station (130W, 6 Ports): https://ankerfast.club/cid4xc
Anker Nano series: https://ankerfast.club/png6ls

Filmed entirely in Paris, this is a full real-world camera comparison covering photography, video, zoom, portraits, and everyday usability.

Gear I used in this video:
Freewell x StevenDivish Anamorphic Lens - http://bit.ly/4hgBHu7 (use code: STEVENXFREEWELL for discount)
iPhone 17 Pro Max Case - http://bit.ly/49cwalY
Tripod - http://bit.ly/4hdnn5e
Phone Grip - http://bit.ly/47bRYfe
All my Gear → https://amzn.to/4eFI6wD

iPhone 17 Pro Anamorphic Lens tutorial video: https://youtu.be/YtV5I0j4khw

The Vivo X300 Pro brings an entirely new Zeiss Photography Kit, complete with a detachable Zeiss telephoto lens, and some of the cleanest color processing I’ve seen. The iPhone 17 Pro, on the other hand, still dominates in video quality and overall consistency.

You’ll see both phones side by side in real lighting conditions, from the streets of Paris to the Louvre (yes, moments right after the heist), and even on top of the Eiffel Tower. 
This test is focused on real results, not lab specs.

Watch until the end to see which one comes out on top and whether Vivo can finally challenge Apple in the flagship camera space.

snippet.title: FIX iPhone Battery DRAIN After iOS 26.1 UPDATE (iPhone 17 Pro, Max, 16, 15, 14, 13, 12, 11)
snippet.description:
Is your iPhone battery draining crazy fast after updating to iOS 26? On iPhone 17 Pro, iPhone 17 Pro Max, iPhone 16 (Pro, Max), iPhone 15 (Pro, Max). Don’t worry — in this video, I’m gonna show you all the quick and easy fixes that actually work! From turning off background app refresh to limiting location services and checking battery usage by apps, you’ll learn exactly how to stop battery drain and boost performance instantly.

Watch till the end to make sure your iPhone battery lasts all day on iOS 26.

00:00 intro
00:11 Fix #1
00:47 Fix #2
01:08 Fix #3
01:45 Fix #4
02:23 Fix #5
02:44 Fix #6
03:22 Fix #7
03:57 Fix #8
04:08 Fix #9
04:44 Fix #10
05:20 Tips

Here are all quick fixes to stop iPhone battery drain fast. Without wasting your time, let’s jump in.

First, check battery usage. Go to Settings → Battery, and see which apps use the most power. Turn off Background App Refresh for those apps, or set it to Wi-Fi only.

Next, limit Location Services. Go to Settings → Privacy & Security → Location Services, and set apps to “While Using.” Scroll down and turn off Product Improvement and Significant Locations.

Then, fix mail background refresh. Open Settings → Mail → Accounts → Fetch New Data, and set it to Manual. Also, turn off unnecessary app notifications from Settings → Notifications.

Enable Optimized Battery Charging under Settings → Battery → Charging, and set charge limit to 80%. If battery health shows “Service,” it’s time for replacement. Go to Apple’s battery replacement site, get an estimate for your model, and click Get Service to book repair or mail-in.

Enable Adaptive Power Mode and Low Power Mode from Settings or Control Center.

Turn off Always-On Display from Settings → Display & Brightness to save more power.

If something feels off, Reset All Settings from Settings → General → Transfer or Reset iPhone → Reset.

Extra tips: don’t use your phone while charging, keep brightness around 50%, turn on Auto Brightness, and disable 5G in low-signal areas.

Make sure your iPhone’s running the latest iOS 26 update, like 26.0.1.

Bekijk dan onze Goedmakers serie waarin we niet of verkeerd bezorgde pakketjes alsnog bezorgen, maar dan met een upgrade waar je u tegen zegt! Of ben je meer op zoek naar inspiratie? In de sfeermakers serie laten wij jou zien hoe jij het interieur van je dromen krijgt. Heb je cadeaustress voor die kleine bengel van je? In Cadeau Kennertjes wordt het vetste speelgoed vakkundig gekeurd door een groep young professionals tussen de 7 en 9 jaar oud. Zo weet jij precies hoe je punten kunt scoren tijdens Sinterklaas. Kortom, genoeg te beleven hier dus voor handige lifehacks, vette gadgets, en hebberig makende speelgoedinspiratie, ben je op het bol YouTube kanaal aan het juiste adres.

snippet.title: Spigen Tough Armor iPhone 17 Pro Max – Old Design, New Drop Data, Still Awesome (11 of 50)
snippet.description:
The Spigen Tough Armor has been around longer than I’ve been reviewing cases, and after all these years, it still holds up. When a design works, you don’t mess with it — and now I’ve got the drop-test data to prove just how tough it really is.

Get your own Spigen Tough Armor T [MagFit] Compatible with Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max - Black by using this link: https://mreh.ca/3WdZIIu
*This is an Amazon affiliate link that will bring you to the right Amazon store for your location

#ReviewerNotInfluencer #Reviews #iPhone

In this video:
0:00 Intro
0:32 Design
1:43 iPhone Access
2:28 Protective Design
3:01 Drop Protection
4:42 Should you get it?

Spigen Tough Armor T [MagFit] Compatible with Apple iPhone 17 Pro Max - Black Review

-With the iPhone 17 version, Spigen took the high road
-other companies went the lazy route
-the PC back of the case extends around the camera lump
-think this has any benefit in terms of protection?
-you still have the tiny borderline useless kickstand
-iPhone sits on a bed of yellow foam that's tucked into a TPU frame
-wrapped with a PC frame
-case seems to get slicker every year
-Spigen doesn't use the tacky soft rubber coating anymore
-even the TPU is really smooth
-the only saving grace is the edge is about the thickness of the iPhone so the case doesn't feel like a drop risk
-its' the same story as the Air, this is the only tough case that doesn't feel like a tough case on this device because of the edge
-weighing 58g, feels lighter than it looks
-average case is 45g
-going to make your iPhone bigger by about 65%
-for corner drops
-initial impact to 9.7G's, that's better by 7%
-the rate of rotation after the intial drop is higher by about 20%
-most likely due to the impact foam
-oddly enough, this increase in spin didn't have much of an negative impact because
-the rate at which the case settles was better by 45%
-and the total force is better by about 8%
-BUT it takes a bit longer for the entire case to settle down

-in short, the Spigen Tough Armor does an amazing job protecting your iPhone
-during corner drops because...

-face first drops might be an issue
-the case didn't fail
-but the intial impact was about 5% higher
-the selling rate was 34% worse
-but the total force was about 11 better because the case just stopped moving really quickly
-still doesn't negate the bigger initial impact but it's not a complete blow out

-for the camera lump
-it is covered
-because Spigen went high with the PC back
-the intial impact was 10% better
-took a lot longer to settle because of the PC back
-and the total force was 23% lower than average
-pretty good
-it would be tough to do but it would be neat to see how much better the Tough ARmor would be if Spigen added some of the foam around the camera lump 
-the corners of this case are 3.2mm thick
-a hair thinner than the Beats Rugged I reviewed two videos ago
-but unlike the Beats Rugged
-the back is just as thick at 3.2mm

-the corners have Spigen's impact foam in the corners which is good to see
-there were a couple of years where the foam wasn't in the corners which didn't make sense
-the inside of the case looks assymetical in terms of foam layout because of the kickstand
-don't stare at it too long or else your brain starts to hurt

-for rigidity, it took 16N to bend 8mm
-that's 46% below average 
-the case does have a PC back but it's spotty along the edges
-so there is a lot of give
-is this going to be an issue for protection?
-my initial impression was yes because the first 10 cases I tested, the ones with rigid backs did better
-for magsafe, took 9.2N to pull and 11.7 to push
-these are very average values
-and again, higher than Apple's own cases
-in general, I dislike cases with integrated buttons because it's a cope-out in terms of case design
-BUT Spigen does it well with the Tough Armor
-because the integrated buttons are the width of the case edge so they're big and  easy to find and press
-this case also has the largest Action button on a case 
-and it has a different finish so it's super easy to find
-there's an actual camera control button, unlike the next spigen case I'm going to review
-for screen access, the edges are quite low
-don't get in the way of swiping
